description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a rectangular wooden cabinet and a large, convex glass screen dominating the front. The front panel features a silver-gray bezel framing the screen, with a vertical control section on the right that includes rotary dials, push buttons, and a speaker grille. The sides and top are finished in a wood veneer, giving it a classic mid-20th-century appearance. The back is made of black plastic with ventilation slats and a protruding section housing the cathode ray tube and internal electronics. The television sits on small feet, elevating it slightly above the surface. This design is typical of analog televisions from the 1970s or 1980s, often found in living rooms, garages, or curbside in urban environments.
description: A vintage wooden-cased television with a large convex glass screen, silver bezel, and side control panel featuring dials and buttons. The back is black plastic with ventilation and a protruding CRT housing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large convex screen, silver bezel, and right-side control panel with dials, buttons, and speaker grille.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the wood veneer finish and the depth of the television, with the CRT bulge visible at the back.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the black plastic back panel, ventilation slats, and the protruding CRT housing.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the wood veneer and the CRT bulge, mirroring the left side.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick silver-gray bezel, rectangular, plastic material, with a classic vintage style.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of rotary dials and push buttons on the front right vertical panel.
